originalBehavior: The route opens with content notes, loading overlays, click-to-advance hints, and an intro scene before the main branching story begins.
localStatus: The content-note, loading, pause, and intro flow remain intact, with only the public framing copy neutralized for the local route.
notes: The story still starts from the same staged overlay and intro sequencing as upstream.
The local cleanup changes visible copy but not the gating behavior for the opening notes.
evidence: Local smoke testing on 2026-03-10 confirmed the route still opens with the content note and progresses into the story scene stack.
Source structure keeps the original dedicated loader and content-note modules.

originalBehavior: Players advance through branching dialogue, timing beats, and choice prompts that change how the anxiety voice escalates or backs off.
localStatus: Dialogue sequencing and choice flow remain in the original scene runtime, with no rewrite of the act scripts or timing helpers.
notes: The local port preserves the branching structure and button timing that define the story-game pacing.
Public cleanup was confined to shell copy and sharing markup.
evidence: The recheck plan explicitly called out branching dialogue and options flow as parity targets.
The vendored scene files still divide narrative logic across the original act modules.

originalBehavior: The story tracks anxiety or health state changes through the HUD and reacts to player decisions across several acts.
localStatus: State changes and HUD behavior remain upstream-driven through the original HP and scene systems.
notes: The route still uses the original health meter and scene-state triggers.
No local changes were made to the main anxiety or recovery mechanics.
evidence: Smoke review included decision branches and HP changes because they anchor the story's feedback loop.
The imported runtime still wires those systems through the original game and act scripts.

originalBehavior: Players can pause, adjust text speed, switch automatic advance behavior, and control audio volume while the story is running.
localStatus: Options, pause behavior, and pacing controls stay in the original UI scripts and remain available through the same gear-driven overlay.
notes: The shell cleanup keeps these controls visible because they are core to accessibility and pacing.
The about panel now focuses on replay and support resources instead of promo links.
evidence: The recheck plan specifically called out pause and content-note overlays as required behaviors.
Current source still centers these controls in the original game UI modules.

originalBehavior: The story concludes with an ending sequence, then offers support resources, replay, and post-story navigation options.
localStatus: Replay, close, and resource handoff still exist, while self-promo and supporter-facing about content was removed from the public overlay.
notes: The local route keeps the mental-health resource link live as part of the story's subject-matter handoff.
Replay remains surfaced through the original about overlay container.
evidence: End-state review verified that the route still reaches the ending and exposes replay behavior.
The credits and about modules remain vendored from the published runtime.

originalBehavior: Players can share the story through social links and open a separate media pack containing trailer, GIF, and screenshot assets.
localStatus: Share links now recalculate from the local subpath, and the media-pack page remains reachable with the shared footer layered on top.
notes: Static upstream share URLs were replaced with neutral local placeholders before JS enhancement runs.
The local media-pack route stays available as a public subpage under the same footer-only reference policy.
evidence: The recheck plan explicitly called out corrected local share behavior and any new subpages.
Current source rewrites share URLs from window.location and retains the shipped media assets.
